title: France Slashes Vaccine Booster Interval to Just THREE Months
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, inflammatory language ('gene therapy injection,' 'draconian,' 'medical apartheid'), conspiracy framing ('transnational pharmaceutical cartels'), selective misrepresentation of vaccine efficacy data, and dismissal of legitimate health concerns without presenting opposing scientific evidence.

title: FLASHBACK What Really Happened at Jonestown
justification: Strong bias with conspiracy framing, unverifiable claims presented as fact (CIA involvement, 'experimental offshore jungle prison camp'), dismissive language ('manufactured lie', 'liar-vision'), selective presentation of Jonestown events, and inflammatory tone suggesting deliberate government deception without credible evidence.

title: Super Tuesday Exposes An Illusion of Democracy in America
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('establishment figure,' 'regime vice president,' 'rigged,' 'stolen nomination,' 'manipulation'), selective fact presentation, dismissive treatment of opposing views, and inflammatory framing that presents contested political claims as established fact.

title: Iranian Oil Tankers Due in Venezuelan Port, With Air force and Naval Escort
justification: The article presents factual events (Iranian tankers, Venezuelan escort, US sanctions) but exhibits subtle bias through selective framing: emphasizing US threats and sanctions while presenting Venezuelan/Iranian perspectives favorably, using charged language like 'self-styled' for Guaidó, and omitting counterarguments about the tankers' purpose or US security concerns.

title: FLASHBACK: When the MMR Autism Controversy Began
justification: Strong bias evident through selective framing that suggests vaccine-autism link is plausible without scientific basis; uses loaded language ('debilitating condition,' 'aggressive program,' 'potent vaccinations') and presents risen autism diagnosis rates as evidence of harm while omitting explanation that diagnostic criteria expansion and increased awareness account for increases; emphasizes controversy and 'one British researcher' (Andrew Wakefield's fraudulent study) without clearly stating the link was thoroughly debunked.

title: Syrian Army to ISIS Surrender or die in East Sweida
justification: Article contains subtle bias through selective framing that favors the Syrian Arab Army perspective (presenting their demands as final, emphasizing ISIS being 'fully besieged'), while the extensive copyright redactions make verification impossible and create an incomplete narrative that obscures context.

title: All Eyes on Idlib Rebel Terrorists Brace for Impending Offensive
justification: Strong bias evident through inflammatory language ('rebel terrorists,' 'enablers,' 'foregone conclusion'), selective framing that demonizes one side while presenting Syrian/Russian actions neutrally, and omission of context about opposition groups' concerns or civilian impact beyond displacement numbers.

title: Pharma Anschluss: Austria Considering Policy of State Segregation, House Arrest for Unvaccinated
justification: Strong bias with charged inflammatory language ('Pharma Anschluss,' 'experimental corporate injection,' 'medical apartheid,' historical Nazi comparison), selective framing that portrays public health policy as authoritarian oppression, misleading characterization of vaccines as 'experimental,' omission of scientific context, and advocacy language designed to delegitimize government policy.

title: UN Report: 50 million People Trapped in 'Modern Slavery'
justification: The article contains subtle bias through selective framing and editorial voice ('woke,' 'continue to go relatively unnoticed') that favors one perspective, while presenting factual UN report data; the opening paragraph's political commentary lacks balance but the core reporting remains grounded in verifiable sources.

title: Germany Pushes Back on Trump: Cold War U.S. 'Nuclear Shield' Deal Now in Jeopardy
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('spurious claim,' 'rogue administration,' 'neoconservative'), selective framing that portrays Trump administration as aggressive while presenting Russia's position sympathetically, omission of specific German government responses, and conspiracy-laden framing of U.S. strategy as intentional sabotage.

title: VIDEO CHIPs Akbar Dubai Police Unveil New Hoverbike But Cant Get Lift-off
justification: Strong bias with sensationalized framing ('science fiction,' mocking tone about 'jockeys'), selective presentation of failures without context, and inflammatory language suggesting incompetence; combined with extensive copyright redactions obscuring actual content makes verification impossible.

title: Pharma's Deception: 'Safe and Effective' - Then and Now
justification: Strong bias with selective presentation of thalidomide history to suggest current pharmaceutical companies will 'repeat the same crimes,' using charged language ('Deception,' 'crimes') and drawing unsupported equivalencies between historical tragedy and modern pharmaceuticals without substantive evidence presented in the article itself.

title: 6 False Claims Made by Media About Assange & WikiLeaks in Relation to Mueller's Russia Investigation
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that favors WikiLeaks/Assange while characterizing media coverage as 'falsehoods,' 'misinformation,' and a 'witch hunt'; omits substantive counterarguments and relies heavily on WikiLeaks' own claims without independent verification.

title: Syrian Army Secure Oil Field Near Northeastern Border with Turkey
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('NATO and their terrorists', 'invasion', 'separatist dreams'), selective narrative framing that portrays one side as heroic while dismissing Kurdish forces and Western allies, missing context on the geopolitical situation, and editorial voice throughout rather than factual reporting.

title: Elon Musk Purchases 9.2% Stake in Twitter, Now Single Largest Shareholder
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing that emphasizes Musk's criticisms of Twitter's 'toxic culture of censorship' and 'free speech' concerns while omitting counterarguments or balanced perspectives on content moderation; uses charged language like 'railed against' and 'severely damaged' to favor Musk's narrative.

title: INTERVIEW: The REAL Anthony Fauci with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('biblical plague,' 'hijacked,' 'cartel,' 'cynical euphemism'), selective facts omitting counterarguments, and misleading framing that portrays Fauci as corrupt without presenting opposing perspectives or substantive evidence.

title: 2020 Election Roundup: Does Anybody Really Care Right Now?
justification: Moderate bias evident through loaded language ('billionaire stalking horse,' 'Sleepy Joe,' 'that's what Bernie does'), dismissive framing ('Does Anybody Really Care'), selective presentation of facts, and editorial commentary masked as news reporting, particularly the sarcastic treatment of candidates and the author's opinion inserted throughout.

title: Ten Years Ago Today: The Slaughter of Libya and Assassination of Gaddafi
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('slaughter,' 'assassination,' 'scam,' 'gloating'), selective cherry-picking of facts (praising Gaddafi's social programs while omitting his authoritarian brutality), and one-sided framing that presents complex geopolitical events as simple US malfeasance without presenting opposing perspectives or counterarguments.

title: Levin on FBI Raiding Trump Home: 'The FBI is Corrupt'
justification: Moderate bias: The article heavily emphasizes Levin's critical perspective on the FBI raid with inflammatory language ("worst attack on this republic in modern history," "outrageous") while providing minimal counterargument or context; Trump's perspective is centered while alternative viewpoints about the legality or necessity of the investigation are largely absent or minimized.

title: BREAKING: Epstein Found Dead in Jail After Apparent Hanging Suicide
justification: Moderate bias through selective framing and suggestive language: the article implies a suspicious conspiracy ('many powerful and well-connected individuals...who would like nothing more than to see this case buried' and 'will certainly help the cause of the rich and powerful') without evidence, uses loaded phrasing like 'untimely death,' and emphasizes the timing of document releases to suggest foul play, while presenting speculation as fact.

title: Long-detained immigrant families could soon face deportation
justification: The article contains subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: it highlights concerns from lawyers and advocacy groups about imminent deportations while presenting ICE's position minimally, uses phrases like 'long-detained' and 'families' repeatedly to emphasize sympathy, and focuses on Trump-era policies as context without balancing explanation of Biden administration's rationale for enforcement decisions.

title: Five things to know about California and other wildfires
justification: Nearly neutral article that presents multiple perspectives on wildfire causes (forest management, climate change, wind/temperature) with balanced attribution to experts and officials, though the framing slightly favors climate science perspective by positioning it as broader context while noting the administration's forest management approach separately.

title: Police investigate arrest of Black woman seen in viral video
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with objective language and balanced presentation of facts; minor framing choices like leading with 'racial' context and the Brooks comparison at the end slightly emphasize one perspective, but the article presents the incident factually without significant editorializing.

title: Sen Mazie Hironos message to American men Just shut up and step up
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article prominently features Hirono's charged language ('just shut up and step up') directed at men generally, while presenting Ford's allegations in detail but Kavanaugh's denials more briefly, and emphasizing the supportive letter from women without equal weight to Ford's account.

title: Inmate: Man accused of killing fiancee wanted witness dead
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; presents facts of trial testimony, prosecution evidence, and defense challenges in balanced manner, though slight emphasis on prosecution's 'explosive testimony' suggests subtle framing favoring the prosecution side.

title: Florida zoo: Man injured by jaguar after crossing barrier
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with factual information about the incident; minimal editorial framing, though the phrase 'taunting the jaguar' slightly characterizes the man's behavior. Word choice is objective overall, presenting facts straightforwardly without clear bias toward either party.

title: Parents of boy who fatally shot sister charged with neglect
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with objective language and factual details; minimal word choice bias, though 'deplorable' in the officer's quote and emphasis on tragic details slightly favor a critical perspective of the parents without editorializing.

title: US accuses Iran of defying UN resolution endorsing nuke deal
justification: Subtle bias shown through selective framing that emphasizes U.S. accusations and Iranian violations while presenting Iran's denials minimally; the description of Revolutionary Guard Corps as 'terrorist organization' uses charged language, and the article focuses primarily on U.S. allegations without substantial counterarguments or Iranian perspective.

title: Trump admin touts childrens health programs after ousting top scientist
justification: The article contains subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the timing of the announcement (immediately after Etzel's placement on leave) is presented to suggest optics-driven motivation, the director's ouster is emphasized early and repeatedly, and skeptical voices from advocacy groups are given prominent space while the administration's explanation is briefly presented and dismissed; however, multiple perspectives are included and language remains largely neutral.

title: Denver neighborhood with ties to KKK recommends name change
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; presents both the neighborhood's recommendation for name change and opposing viewpoints (previous referendum result, Walker Stapleton's concerns about democratic process), though slightly emphasizes social justice arguments through selective quotes and framing around George Floyd's death.

title: Execs: US internet casino gambling is poised for expansion
justification: Nearly neutral article with objective reporting on online casino gambling expansion, though minor framing favors industry optimism and growth narrative without equally emphasizing regulatory concerns or consumer protection issues.

title: Man charged in death of Michigan woman missing since 2005
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; straightforward presentation of facts about charges, suspect, and victim details, with only very slight editorial framing in the inclusion of the sister's emotional quote which adds context but remains factually grounded.

title: Yosemite wildfire threatens grove of iconic sequoia trees
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with factual information about the wildfire threat to Yosemite's sequoia groves; minor word choices like 'largest grove' and 'iconic trees' are slightly emphatic but appropriate to the subject matter, and multiple perspectives from officials are included.

title: Feds look into treatment of mentally ill adults in Oklahoma
justification: Article is purely factual and neutral, reporting on a Justice Department civil rights investigation with direct quotes from officials, specific references to the 1999 Supreme Court ruling, named organizations, and balanced presentation of statements from state and local authorities.

title: South Dakota woman convicted in her baby's 1981 death
justification: Article is factual and neutral, reporting specific details of a verified cold case conviction with names, dates, locations, and procedural steps presented objectively without editorial framing or bias.

title: Potential jurors see racism behind Ahmaud Arbery's slaying
justification: Nearly neutral reporting on jury selection statements with minimal bias; presents both prosecution perspectives (potential jurors' racial profiling claims) and defense arguments (self-defense claim, suspicion of crimes) with only subtle framing favoring the prosecution narrative through selective emphasis on damaging juror statements.

title: Oklahoma City police kill inmate who took officer hostage
justification: Article presents factual information about the incident with neutral language, reports statements from named officials (Sheriff Tommie Johnson III and Police Chief Wade Gourley), and includes context without editorial commentary or bias toward either perspective.

title: Police believe 3 children drowned by mother at NYC beach
justification: The article is factual and neutral, reporting details of the incident with objective language, presenting statements from police officials and family members without editorial bias or charged framing.

title: Florida GOP lawmakers pass bill to limit discussion of race
justification: Subtle bias through framing and word choice: phrases like 'political culture war' (in quotes but still framing), emphasis on Black senators' opposition while noting 'little Republican debate for it,' selective quoting of Democratic criticism, and the statement that there is 'little to no evidence' that CRT is taught in K-12 schools subtly undermine the bill's stated rationale without presenting Republican counterarguments equally.

title: When Guns Are Outlawed Only Outlaws Will Have Guns
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through selective statistical framing, loaded language ('propaganda,' 'fixation'), rhetorical questions designed to dismiss gun violence concerns, cherry-picked data comparisons that minimize firearms deaths, and an explicit editorial agenda questioning 'the real agenda of gun ban advocates' rather than presenting balanced perspectives on gun policy.

title: Twitter Users Flood Epilepsy Foundation Page with Seizure-Triggering Posts
justification: Strong bias with selective framing and unsubstantiated claims: the article conflates a documented attack on the Epilepsy Foundation's Twitter with speculative health claims about blue light and cell phone radiation that lack scientific support, using charged language like 'scary trend' and cherry-picking information while omitting expert consensus on radiation safety.

title: Living In Florida Becomes More Expensive As Insurers Jack Up Rates By Double-Digits
justification: Article contains subtle bias through selective framing and word choices like 'jack up rates' and 'out of control' that favor one perspective, while multiple quotes from officials and experts provide some balance; however, there is emphasis on the problem without exploring counterarguments or insurance industry justifications.

title: Predictive Algorithms Are No Better At Telling The Future Than A Crystal Ball
justification: Article presents a critical perspective on predictive algorithms with selective emphasis on flaws and limitations, using charged language like 'mythical' and 'crystal ball,' while omitting counterarguments about algorithmic benefits or successful implementations.

title: Supreme Court on Mandates: A Little Good News and a Bunch of Bad News
justification: Strong bias evident through inflammatory framing ('a bunch of bad news,' 'not a time to close') and selective emphasis on negative aspects of Supreme Court rulings without presenting balanced analysis or opposing viewpoints; article structure suggests advocacy rather than objective reporting.

title: Dear Diabetics Videotaped Research Study Demonstrates How Using Treadmills Can Mess With Your Blood
justification: Strong bias with misleading framing, cherry-picked evidence, and unsubstantiated claims presented as established fact; uses charged language like 'Electrosmog' and 'Microwave Sickness' without scientific consensus, omits substantial peer-reviewed research contradicting these claims, and makes sweeping health assertions unsupported by mainstream medical evidence.

title: As State Censorship Grows, VPN Usage Soars During COVID-19 Crisis
justification: The article presents VPN usage statistics with selective framing that emphasizes state censorship in the headline without substantial evidence within the text; the title suggests censorship as a primary driver of VPN adoption, but the article itself attributes growth mainly to lockdowns and remote work, creating a disconnect between the sensationalized headline and the actual content presented.

title: Deutsche Bank Collapse Could Crash Global Financial Markets
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing that emphasizes systemic risk and bank failures while omitting counterarguments about Deutsche Bank's recovery efforts; uses alarmist language ('crash global financial markets,' 'bleeding') and cherry-picks negative facts without balancing context about regulatory safeguards or the bank's stabilization measures.

title: How Close Are We To A War On Iran?
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('hardline tactics,' 'sanctions war,' 'make things worse'), one-sided framing that portrays US actions negatively while presenting Iranian perspective sympathetically, dismissal of Western media as merely 'following along with US opinions,' and incomplete sentences suggest editorial manipulation or poor editing that obscures the full argument.

title: Did the Economy Just Stumble Off a Cliff?
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective use of data, unsubstantiated claims ('gut feeling,' 'disturbance in the Force'), and framing that emphasizes economic weakness while dismissing positive metrics as misleading without rigorous evidence; relies heavily on anecdotal observations rather than comprehensive analysis.

title: Elizabeth Warren Introduces Bill to Bring Back One of the Federal Government's Worst Pandemic Policies
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('unjustifiable overreach,' 'destroyed,' 'bankrupted'), selective presentation of facts omitting counterarguments, and misleading framing that dismisses progressive concerns as 'misconceptions' without substantive rebuttal.

title: Like Fitbits Data-Collecting Baby Monitors Not Accurate And Also Emit Harmful Wi-Fi Radiation EMF
justification: Strong bias with selective use of facts, misleading framing equating correlation with causation (rashes and radiation), unsubstantiated claims about 'life-threatening symptoms,' and inflammatory language ('Big Wireless,' 'nasty stuff') that advocates against products while omitting counterarguments from manufacturers or regulatory bodies.

title: New "Emergency Measures" Will Come From The QUARANTINE ACT - It's Not Looking Good For Canada
justification: Strong bias with inflammatory language ('sinister act', 'completely obliterate'), unsubstantiated claims about government intentions presented as fact, selective omission of context about actual legal provisions, and alarmist framing equating public health measures to 'martial law' without evidence or counterargument.

title: Experts Question Whether It's Legal That Utilities Can Spy on Customers with "Smart" Meters
justification: Strong bias through inflammatory language ('reprehensible'), selective citation without substantiation, cherry-picked claims about fires/explosions and health issues presented as established fact, consistent one-sided framing dismissing smart meters entirely while omitting legitimate benefits or counterarguments, and alarmist speculation about surveillance without balanced presentation of actual regulatory frameworks.

title: Trial Facial Recognition Technology To Be Used In UK At Select Self-Checkouts
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article leads with convenience framing for age verification while emphasizing privacy concerns and surveillance expansion in later sections, creates negative tone with 'worrying prospect' language, and omits counterarguments about security benefits or regulatory compliance requirements.

title: It's "Unreasonable" for Banks to Share Your Financial Info With the Government, 8 in 10 Americans Say
justification: The article reports survey findings from a verifiable Cato Institute/YouGov poll with specific methodology details, maintains neutral language, and presents data without overt editorializing, though the headline's framing of the survey result as 'unreasonable' adopts the survey's language rather than remaining purely objective.

title: Rebuilding US Leading Role Is The Key for European Security, British Experts
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('mirage', 'hysteria', 'flimsy'), selective presentation of leaked documents to support an anti-Western narrative, dismissal of security concerns as propaganda, and framing that presents complex geopolitical issues as deliberately manufactured by mass media.

title: FDA Ordered Google to Remove Covid Videos from YouTube Because Agency Didn't Like Their Message
justification: Strong bias with selective presentation and charged language ('plandemic', 'demanded censorship'); omits context about FDA regulatory authority over health claims; presents unverified narrative about FDA coercion without independent corroboration; uses inflammatory framing ('How often the FDA has made other censorship demands') while acknowledging lack of evidence.

title: Flea Collars Linked to Thousands of Pet Deaths and Injuries to Pets AND Humans - EPA Still Won't Warn Public
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing (emphasizing EPA inaction and failure to warn), cherry-picked narrative focus on harm cases, charged language ('rammed through' framing via editorial tone), and omission of counterarguments such as regulatory approval rationale, safety data supporting the product, or veterinary guidance on proper use.

title: Opening the Door for Censorship: New Trademark Enforcement Mechanisms Added for Top-Level Domains
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing favoring nonprofit/civil society perspective; uses phrases like 'Sadly' and 'rammed through' language ('requiring'), omits trademark holders' legitimate concerns about cybersquatting protection, presents opposition as 'nearly unanimous' without verification, and characterizes the policy primarily through criticism without balanced analysis of enforcement benefits.

title: WATCH: Dan Rather Explains How Close We Actually Are To A Republican Defection From Trump
justification: Article presents Dan Rather's commentary on Republican defection without providing full context or counterarguments; the headline uses sensationalized framing ('How Close We Actually Are') to emphasize conflict, and the censored portions make verification difficult, but the core reporting appears to reference a real CNN appearance.

title: Trump Insanely Tells Kids At Easter Egg Roll That There Is No Name For The White House VIDEO
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('batshit crazy manbaby,' 'insanely'), dismissive framing ('so-called president'), selective presentation of facts, and editorializing throughout that advocates against Trump rather than reporting neutrally on his Easter Egg Roll remarks.

title: Responsible Gun Owner Drunkenly Fires New Toy Almost Killing A Baby A Half-Mile Away
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through inflammatory language ('almost kill,' 'near-murder,' 'rammed through'), loaded framing that mocks the NRA rather than objectively reporting the incident, and selective emphasis on the 'responsible gun owner' narrative with sarcasm, while presenting one perspective without balanced counterargument.

title: Nervous Jill Stein Defends Russia While Withholding Important Documents From Mueller
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('doozie,' 'apparently forgetting the mountain of potential evidence shes hiding'), editorializing ('generally doesnt care about facts'), dismissive framing of Stein's civil liberties argument, and selective quotation that undermines her position while the author's commentary suggests bad faith.

title: Team Trump Knew Flynn Broke The Law And Hired Him Anyway (DETAILS)
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('Team Trump knew Flynn broke the law'), selective presentation of facts, and misleading framing that assumes guilt without adequate context or counterarguments; multiple copyright redactions suggest incomplete sourcing.

title: Trump Jr Mocked To Oblivion After Democracy Dies In Dankness Blunder TWEETS
justification: Article is largely incoherent with extensive redacted sections, making it impossible to verify claims; contains inflammatory language ('Democracy Dies in Dankness,' 'dank') and appears to be intentional disinformation or spam rather than genuine journalism.

title: The FBI's Russia Investigation Just Took A Turn That Will TERRIFY Trump (DETAILS)
justification: Strong bias evident through inflammatory language ('terrify,' 'cash-grab,' 'betrayal'), loaded framing that presumes guilt ('colluded with Russia,' 'nailing him'), one-sided presentation omitting counterarguments, and selective facts presented as evidence of wrongdoing without substantiation.

title: 'Lie After Lie After Lie': Fox's Shep Smith Breaks Ranks, Goes Nuclear On Team Trump (VIDEO)
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy: inflammatory language ('criminal enterprise,' 'propaganda arm,' 'deluded Trumpkins'), selective presentation of Smith's comments without full context, dismissive framing of opposing views, and editorial appeals to the reader ('Thank you for bringing real news,' 'Hats off to you').

title: Trump F*cks Himself By Admitting Russia Interfered With 2016 Election To Help Him Win
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('f*cks himself,' 'scapegoating,' 'totally illegitimate'), selective framing of Trump's statements, and dismissive treatment of counterarguments; article presents interpretations as fact while using inflammatory rhetoric to attack Trump.

title: Yale Professor Explains Exactly Why We Need To Compare Trump To Hitler
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing: article uses loaded language ('intellectual laziness,' 'white-wash'), presents one expert's perspective as authoritative without counterargument, conflates historical lessons with implicit Trump-Hitler comparison, and frames refusal to make comparisons as problematic rather than presenting balanced debate on whether such comparisons are valid or useful.

title: Obama Photographer BRILLIANTLY Trolls Trump's Wildly Inappropriate Speech To The Boy Scouts (IMAGE)
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy and inflammatory language: 'orange fascist,' 'goons,' 'fraudulent vulgarian,' and dehumanizing characterizations; selective presentation of facts omitting any counterarguments or context for Trump's speech; extreme disparagement of Trump paired with hagiographic treatment of Obama without balance.

title: Like Talking To A Toddler School Shooting Victims Mom Recalls Meeting With Trump
justification: Strong bias evident through inflammatory language ('toddler,' 'very fine people' as sarcasm, 'wacky'), selective framing that portrays Trump negatively, dismissal of opposing views ('idea we have already seen is terrible'), and cherry-picked quotes without full context or counterarguments.

title: Former McCain Chief Of Staff Thinks U.S. Needs A Democratic Congress
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('things just keep getting stranger,' 'nothing short of extraordinary,' 'mean to Hillary'), rhetorical questions designed to mock the administration, selective framing of events, and dismissive characterization of the White House explanation as implausible without presenting counterarguments or full context.

title: Trump Just Sent A MAJOR F*ck You To The Supreme Court, CONFIRMS His Own Incompetency (DETAILS)
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('F*ck You,' 'can't get their sh*t together,' 'rude, disorganized'), sweeping inflammatory generalizations ('most incompetent, lazy, and disorganized, but it is the most corrupt'), and selective framing that presents only critical perspective while omitting Trump administration's counterarguments or context.

title: OOPS Fox News Accidentally Published Story Announcing McCabes Firing Hours Before It Happened
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through charged language ('teensy chance,' 'completely political firing,' 'punish'), selective omission of Fox News's explanation, leading rhetorical questions implying guilt without evidence, and a concluding editorial statement attacking Republicans that goes well beyond reporting.

title: 60 Minutes Cut Stormy Daniels Interview To Benefit Trump
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('Stable Genius,' 'deprive'), selective framing presenting unverified claims about editorial decisions as fact, misleading characterization of CBS's motives without evidence, and omission of CBS's editorial reasoning or perspective.

title: Republican Stuns CNN Host, Wants To Starve Americans To Pay For Trump's Border Wall (VIDEO)
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('heartless, revolting', 'disgusting', 'piece of sh*t'), inflammatory framing ('hellbent on destroying', 'moronic'), selective omission of King's full context or counterarguments, and editorializing throughout that advocates against the Republican position rather than reporting neutrally.

title: Responsible Gun Owner Shoots Both Himself And Friend With One Bullet At Utah Gun Show
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through derisive language ('Billy Joe Bob Cretinsburg,' 'ammosexual,' 'cartoonishly'), rhetorical framing that mocks gun owners and the 'good guy with a gun' argument, and selective omission of factual details while inserting editorial commentary rather than reporting neutrally on the incident.

title: Walker-Appointed Judge Just Gave Republican Governor A Huge Middle Finger
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through charged language ('middle finger,' 'rammed through'), selective framing that favors the plaintiff's position, dismissive characterization of Republican efforts ('desperately trying'), and editorial commentary disguised as reporting.

title: Former Breitbart Editor Conservatives Should Start Gunning Journalists Down
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('child molestation advocate,' 'Trump-loving white nationalist,' 'gunning journalists down'), selective presentation of facts without counterarguments, and inflammatory framing designed to delegitimize the subject rather than inform readers objectively.

title: The Defender on FDA's Snail's Pace of Safety Signals in Elderly
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, emotionally charged language ('duty to your elders,' 'should be front page news,' 'quietly disclosed'), and reliance on a single critical source without presenting FDA's or manufacturers' responses or counterarguments regarding safety signal investigation procedures.

title: A Glimpse of America
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('pitchforks and torches,' 'rode hard and put away wet'), selective framing that presents vaccine safety concerns as suppressed truth, dismissal of opposing views as purely political/angry, and one-sided advocacy for vaccine skepticism while omitting substantial scientific consensus on vaccine safety.

title: Boston Globe's Baskin Misses Crucial Questions About Autism and The Future for Families
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('epidemic victims'), and emphasis on system failures while omitting counterarguments or current support initiatives; the article prioritizes one perspective on autism services without balanced presentation of opposing views.

title: CBC Responds to Dr. Lyons-Weiler, Dr. Lyons-Weiler Responds Back
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('all-out attack,' 'defamatory attack,' 'doubled-down'), one-sided presentation of a dispute with CBC, selective framing that presents only Dr. Lyons-Weiler's perspective as factual, unsubstantiated claims about journalistic failures, and advocacy language ('I would encourage everyone to write to CBC') without presenting CBC's actual response or context.

title: San Diego Man Diagnosed with Severe Autism As Tot Defies Prognosis, Passes Bar Exam
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emotional language ('grin from ear to ear,' 'tears of joy,' celebratory tone) that emphasizes inspiration narrative while omitting counterarguments or critical context about autism prevalence, outcomes, or alternative perspectives on the broader autism experience.

title: Happy Father's Day
justification: Article is a personal tribute to the author's father on Father's Day, containing only personal anecdotes, memories, and reflections with no factual claims, statistics, named events, or verifiable references that could be biased or disputed.

title: Ring Doorbell
justification: Article contains subtle bias through selective framing and emotional appeals (e.g., 'It's a joke,' 'The doors are CLOSED') that favor a particular viewpoint about autism services and awareness, though it primarily relates personal anecdotes rather than presenting balanced perspectives on the Ring Doorbell topic.

title: Anthony Fauci Lies then Backtracks on MMR Encephalitis After Audience Gasps at Federal Meeting
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, inflammatory language ('draconian mandate laws', 'worst health crisis EVER'), cherry-picked selective framing of testimony, misleading characterization of Fauci's statements, and unsubstantiated claims presented as fact without evidence.

title: Early-Onset Dementia and Alzheimer's Rates Grow for Younger American Adults
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses emotionally charged framing ('slathered in Purell,' 'struck down by dementia') and selectively emphasizes alarming statistics while omitting important context about what the 200% increase represents in absolute terms, potential diagnostic criteria changes, or alternative explanations for the rise in early-onset dementia diagnoses.

title: Critical Care and Constant Compassion
justification: Article is a personal narrative about a parent's experience with their child's medical procedure; it contains factual reporting of conversations and events with objective language and no detectable bias or editorial stance favoring any particular perspective.

title: This Thanksgiving, I'm Grateful for Statins, Stents and Steroids.
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy against vaccines and the Clinton Foundation, cherry-picked financial data presented without context, conspiratorial framing ('What's the agenda here'), and charged language ('vax-love', 'spell of Neurodiversity') that misrepresents vaccine policy as a financial scheme rather than public health measure.

title: Foo Fighters or Woo Fighters
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('what the hell happened?', 'disheartening in the extreme'), misleading framing equating vaccine requirements to racial segregation, selective omission of public health context, and advocacy language throughout that presents one ideological perspective as fact.

title: Harris County Texas Sheriff's Department Honors and Helps People With Autism
justification: Nearly neutral article sharing a sheriff's department message about autism awareness; minor editorial framing through selective quotes and emphasis on the positive initiative, with minimal opposing perspective, but language remains largely objective and factual.

title: What's More Contagious A Healthy Child without MMR Vaccine or Your Local Ball Play Pit
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('no poop Pumpkin,' sarcastic framing), selective citation of an unstudied claim, dismissive tone toward public health messaging, and a snide insinuation that vaccine manufacturers promote vaccines for profit rather than health reasons.

title: CT Hearing Room Packed Through The Night HB 5044
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('SAME OLD, SAME OLD,' 'waving Dr Wakefield's name like the proverbial bloody shirt'), selective presentation of vaccine safety claims presented as false without substantiation, advocacy framing that dismisses public health officials as uninformed, and omission of scientific consensus on vaccine safety and thimerosal removal.

title: Bill Gates Got A Hall Pass?
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('this jerk', 'insanity'), unsourced claims about 'hall pass' arrangement, selective framing that portrays Gates negatively, inflammatory rhetoric about his influence ('control of the actual planet'), and lacks any counterargument or balanced perspective.

title: Professor Christopher Exley in January 2018 on the "Deafening Response" to his Aluminium in Brain Tissue in Autism Paper
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('attacked,' 'boot on his neck,' 'Wakefielded,' 'tsunami of silence,' 'Big Tobacco playbook'), conspiratorial framing suggesting coordinated media suppression to protect 'vaccine profit,' and selective presentation that portrays Exley's research as suppressed truth while omitting scientific critiques or the broader context of how his work has been evaluated by the scientific community.

title: Breaking FUD
justification: Moderate bias: The article selectively frames mental health disclosures by Olympic athletes as evidence of societal pressure and decline, omits context about their actual statements and circumstances, uses leading questions to impose a narrative about autism and parenting challenges without clear connection to the athletes' situations, and employs rhetorical framing that conflates unrelated issues.

title: How Flattening the Autism Curve Fell.... Flat
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing: the article heavily emphasizes Dr. Zahorodny's statements supporting a 'real' autism increase while omitting substantial scientific consensus, cherry-picks quotes to support the narrative, and uses framing like 'the really lie about autism' that dismisses alternative explanations without balanced counterargument.

title: Citizen Petition and a Petition for a Stay of Action
justification: Strong bias with selective framing and charged language: presents ICAN's demands as fact-based concerns while omitting FDA's scientific rationale, characterizes trial designs as deficient without acknowledging regulatory standards, uses inflammatory language ('alarming deficiencies'), and selectively emphasizes PCR test unreliability without balanced context on trial safety monitoring.

title: Golf's PGA Tour suspends participants in Saudi-backed LIV series
