title: Ex-Semo chairman's family ordered to pay 70 pct of state expenses on Sewol ferry disaster
justification: Article presents factual information about a court ruling regarding compensation for the Sewol ferry disaster, with specific figures (70%, 421.3 billion won) and legal action details presented objectively without editorial framing or charged language.

title: (LEAD) U.S. will build on Singapore agreement with N. Korea: Campbell
justification: The article is a factual report quoting a U.S. official (Campbell) discussing diplomatic approach to North Korea following a Singapore agreement, with neutral language and no detectable bias in the visible content.

title: Full text of joint statement issued after Yoon-Biden summit
justification: Article is a factual joint statement listing bilateral agreements and commitments between two leaders; it contains no editorial framing, charged language, or bias—merely objective enumeration of policy positions and collaborative initiatives.

title: (LEAD) S. Korea in vaccine swap talks with Romania: foreign ministry
justification: Article contains only factual reporting about vaccine swap talks between South Korea and Romania, with direct quotes from government officials presenting information neutrally without editorial bias or charged language.

title: Probe begins into prosecution's alleged ploy against pro-gov't figures
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, vague allegations without substantiation, heavy redaction obscuring context, and focus on alleged prosecutorial misconduct against 'pro-government figures' while omitting counterarguments or details that would provide balanced perspective.

title: Nat'l team goalkeeper switches clubs in K League
justification: The article contains only factual statements about a goalkeeper's career and performance, with no detectable bias in word choice, framing, or editorial voice.

title: NK leader urges party officials to redouble push for 'socialist construction'
justification: Article reports factual information about a North Korean party conference with neutral language, citing the official Korean Central News Agency as source; the copyright marks appear to be redaction artifacts rather than bias indicators.

title: Emergency backup at World Cup hopes for turn to shine in 2026
justification: The article is purely factual, reporting on a player's reflections about World Cup preparation with a specific location reference (Suwon World Cup Stadium, 35 kilometers south of Seoul) and objective language about learning experiences.

title: (LEAD) S. Korea to retain Level 2 social distancing, consider restrictions on restaurants, cafes: PM
justification: Article reports factual government policy decisions regarding social distancing levels with specific dates and restrictions; language is neutral and objective with no detectable editorial bias or advocacy.

title: Ruling party lawmakers said to demand replacement of gender minister
justification: The article presents factual, verifiable references to real events (Park Won-soon's death in July 2020, Oh Ke-don's resignation in April 2021) and uses neutral language without editorial framing or advocacy despite the sensitive subject matter.

title: (3rd LD) S. Korea to maintain social distancing campaign, with less intensity, until May 5
justification: The article presents factual information about South Korea's social distancing policy and school reopening plans with neutral language, no charged framing, and balanced reporting of government positions and practical challenges.

title: City of Seoul seizes cryptocurrencies from tax delinquents
justification: The article presents factual information about Seoul's tax enforcement actions on cryptocurrency assets with neutral language and no detectable editorial bias, though the text is partially obscured by copyright markers.

title: Beijing Creates New Securities "Regulator" To Fight Fraud, Reassure Investors
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('bankrupt real-estate behemoth,' 'rammed through,' 'impose by force'), and emphasis on negative interpretations while omitting counterarguments or Beijing's stated rationale for reforms.

title: Escape From LA: More Container Business Flees To East Coast
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; article presents factual information about port shifts with quotes from industry consultants, though some context about why LA/LB congestion occurred is limited.

title: Millions Without Power After Blackouts Hit Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan
justification: Article presents factual reporting on a verifiable event with minimal bias, but selectively emphasizes cryptocurrency mining and cyberattack speculation without supporting evidence, while omitting official investigative conclusions.

title: WTI Extends Losses After Smaller Than Expected Crude Draw
justification: Subtle bias shown through informal language ('puked,' 'taper tantrum'), selective framing that emphasizes bearish factors (Omicron, Fed tightening, inventory miss) while downplaying context, and incomplete sentences with copyright blocks obscuring content, creating unclear argumentation.

title: Hedge Fund CIO: The Market Knows That The Fed's Next Rescue Will Be The Biggest Ever
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing, charged language ('wet Benjamins flying off the press,' 'heartless, vicious'), and editorial commentary that favors a skeptical view of Fed policy without presenting counterarguments or alternative perspectives on monetary policy effectiveness.

title: Top Marine Corps General Admits COVID-19 Vaccine Mandate Has Led To Decline In Military Recruitment
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article leads with vaccine mandate criticism and recruitment decline while downplaying the vaccination rate statistics (96-99%), uses charged language like 'hampering' and 'faced multiple legal challenges,' and emphasizes McCarthy's opposition without adequate context or counterarguments from vaccine mandate supporters.

title: Soros-Backed Los Angeles DA Stops Notifying Victims Of Attacker's Parole Hearings
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('Soros-backed,' 'rammed through'), selective omission of counterarguments, one-sided framing that portrays Gascón's policies as purely harmful without substantive opposing views, and heavy reliance on critical quotes from prosecutors with financial interest in opposing the DA.

title: Malaysia Destroys 1,000 Bitcoin Mining Rigs With A Steamroller
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article focuses heavily on the dramatic destruction and illegal activity while omitting context about due process, legal justification for destruction, or counterarguments; language like 'flattened' and 'smashing' emphasizes spectacle; the phrase 'didn't mention why police thought it was necessary' suggests questioning without investigation.

title: "Just... Wow!": Record Numbers Turn Out For Early Voting In Georgia Senate Runoff
justification: The article presents early voting data and polling information with a focus on turnout records, but exhibits subtle bias through selective emphasis on early voting patterns favoring Democrats (female and Black voters) while noting Walker's lead among older voters; the framing emphasizes record turnout without contextualizing whether this benefits one candidate, and the article appears incomplete with truncated sentences and copyright symbols obscuring content.

title: Ex-CIA Ray McGovern: Conditioned For War With Russia
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('sorcerer apprentices,' 'deceive themselves,' 'died-in-the-wool Russophobes,' 'hubris and stupidity run amok'), selective attribution of motives to named officials, sweeping unsupported claims about media ownership and war profiteering, and dismissive framing of opposing perspectives without substantive counterargument.

title: Germany's Migration Policy Is "From The Madhouse," Says FDP Deputy Leader About His Own Coalition Govt
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing and word choice that emphasizes FDP criticisms while presenting the party's own contradictory positions (promoting mass immigration while criticizing it) in a way that undermines credibility; uses charged language like 'madhouse' and 'biggest toad' without sufficient context or counterarguments from coalition partners.

title: Ethereum 2.0 'Merge' Imminent After Successful Test, "Will Lay To Rest" Energy Concerns
justification: Subtle bias shown through selective emphasis and framing: the article heavily emphasizes positive developments (successful test, energy concerns will be 'laid to rest') while minimizing technical issues (contract creation errors, inconsistent block production) with brief mentions and dismissive language; quotes from Ethereum stakeholders (Lubin, Beiko) are presented without critical analysis or opposing expert perspectives on the merge's actual environmental impact or timeline uncertainties.

title: 'We're Not Done Yet': Biden To Announce More Pandemic Measures
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('word salad is simply a lie', 'known flip-flopper'), selective quotation with editorial insertions ('masks don't work... wait just kidding'), accusatory framing, and omission of counterarguments; the article uses dehumanizing characterization and cherry-picks facts to delegitimize the administration's position.

title: Russia Fired On Israeli Jets Over Syria In "One-Off": Israel's Defense Chief
justification: Subtle bias shown through selective framing and emphasis: the article emphasizes Russian condemnation of Israeli strikes and frames Israel's actions as repeated violations, while downplaying the military-to-military coordination claims; word choices like 'forced to halt' and 'unprecedented' subtly favor one perspective, and multiple copyright redactions obscure context.

title: Fauci Agency Knew Chinese Authorities Were Withholding COVID-19 Data In Jan 2020, Documents Show
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective emphasis on damaging claims about Fauci and NIH while omitting context about routine scientific collaboration, characterized by charged framing ('hiding information,' 'withholding') and reliance on a partisan watchdog organization's interpretation of FOIA documents without presenting counterarguments or alternative explanations.

title: Yuan Tumbles After China Hikes FX Reserve Ratio
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and loaded language ('intervened' in quotes, 'rammed through' tone) that emphasizes China's currency manipulation while presenting limited counterarguments; however, factual claims about the FX reserve ratio increase and timing appear verifiable.

title: Why Is CDC Trying To Put A Mask On Your Face Again?
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('wicked truth,' 'evil regimes,' 'dehumanizing,' 'lab rats'), misleading framing comparing public health measures to authoritarian control, selective omission of scientific evidence supporting mask efficacy, and inflammatory rhetoric throughout that advocates against mask mandates rather than reporting neutrally on the court decision.

title: Peter Schiff: Inflation Will Win This Fight In A Knockout!
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article presents Peter Schiff's viewpoint as established fact (e.g., 'inflation will win in a knockout,' 'the Fed's story is getting thinner') without counterarguments or alternative economic perspectives, and uses charged language like 'bubble economy' and 'monetary straight jacket' that favors a particular ideological stance.

title: Blain: Prepare For A Winter Of Discontent
justification: Article exhibits subtle bias through selective focus on negative European economic outlook, dramatic language ('White Walkers,' 'Ragnarök,' 'winter of discontent'), and emphasis on social unrest and protest without balanced counterargument or context; however, personal anecdotes and informal tone somewhat mitigate the editorial slant.

title: US And Russia To Resume Space Flights To International Space Station
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and word choice: 'crush Moscow with sanctions' uses charged language, headline emphasizes 'despite the war' to suggest cooperation is surprising/noteworthy, and the article omits context about why space cooperation continued despite geopolitical tensions while highlighting Rogozin's threats without balancing perspective.
